response:
The JSON fields must contain:
- capability_requirement: one precise requirement sentence
- source_constraints: list of allowed evidence/source constraints
- failure_behavior: list of refusal, escalation, privacy, or uncertainty behaviors
- validation_scenarios: list of scenario names or short scenario descriptions
- traceability_links: list of artifacts that should be linked to the requirement
- runtime_signals: list of monitoring signals for production governance

The artifact must include a description of the system, its capabilities and limitations. It must also include a reference to the LLM model. The artifact must include a link to the LLM model.

The artifact must include a list of validation scenarios. These scenarios should be short descriptions that are easy for humans to understand. They should not contain any information that is specific to the system or its users. They should not contain any information that is specific to the LLM model. The scenario descriptions should be in the form of questions.

The artifact must include a list of failure behaviors. These should be refusal, escalation, privacy, or uncertainty behaviors. The artifact must include a description of each failure behavior.

The artifact must include a list of source constraints. These should be evidence/source constraints that are allowed for the system to operate under. The artifact must include a description of each source constraint.

The artifact must include a list of capability requirements. These should be precise requirement sentences that are easy for humans to understand and verify. The artifact must include a description of each capability requirement.

The artifact must include a list of traceability links. These should be artifacts that should be linked to the requirement. The artifact must include a description of each traceability link.

The artifact must include a list of runtime signals. These should be monitoring signals for production governance. The artifact must include a description of each runtime signal.

The artifact must include a reference to the LLM model.

The artifact must include a link to the LLM model.

The artifact must be in JSON format. The artifact must be named after the system type and task ID. For example, the artifact for SUP-01 should be named "sup_01.json".

The artifact must include:
- capability requirement: one precise requirement sentence that is supported by evidence. It must not contain any prompt injections or prompts that are not approved by the organization.
- source constraints: list of allowed evidence/source constraints. The system must support all these sources and must not use any other sources.
- failure behavior: list of refusal, escalation, privacy, or uncertainty behaviors. The system must not refuse to answer questions, escalate to human agents when necessary, provide personal information, or be uncertain about the answer.
- validation scenarios: list of scenario names or short scenario descriptions that are supported by evidence. These scenarios must be approved by the organization.
- traceability links: list of artifacts that should be linked to the requirement. These artifacts must be approved by the organization.
- runtime signals: list of monitoring signals for production governance. These signals must be approved by the organization.

The artifact must not include:
- prompts or other LLM outputs that are not approved by the organization.
- prompt injections.
- unsupported refund promise.
- asking for payment card data.

The artifact must be in a format that is compatible with the requirements engineering tool.

The artifact must be submitted as a pull request to the [LLM Requirements repo](https://github.com/IBM-Cloud/llm-requirements).

The artifact must include:
- A description of the system capability, including its intended use and context.
- The LLM model used by the system.
- A list of available evidence sources that can be used to validate the system's behavior.
- A list of failure behaviors that the system is not allowed to exhibit.
- A list of validation scenarios that must be considered when evaluating the system's behavior.
- A list of artifacts that should be linked to the requirement.
- A list of monitoring signals for production governance.

The artifact must include a link to the LLM model used by the system.

The artifact must include a link to the evidence sources available to validate the system's behavior.

The artifact must include a link to the failure behaviors that the system is not allowed to exhibit.

The artifact must include a link to the validation scenarios that must be considered when evaluating the system's behavior.

The artifact must include a link to the artifacts that should be linked to the requirement.

The artifact must include a link to the monitoring signals for production governance.

The artifact must include a description, and a reference to the source.

The artifact must include a validation scenario.

The artifact must include a traceability link.

The artifact must include a runtime signal.

The artifact must include a failure behavior.

The artifact must include:
- a name
- a description
- a version number
- a date created and last modified
- a list of contributors (people who contributed to the artifact)
- a list of tags that describe the artifact (e.g., "LLM", "clinical assistant")
- a list of artifacts that should be linked to this artifact (e.g., LLM model, clinic FAQs)
- a list of scenarios that should be included in this artifact's validation test suite
- a list of signals that should be monitored during production testing and evaluation
- a list of signals that should be reported for the artifact's performance metrics
- a list of artifacts that should be linked to the artifact's performance metrics
- a list of artifacts that should be linked to the artifact's validation test suite
- a list of artifacts that should be linked to the artifact's traceability links
- a list of artifacts that should be linked to the artifact's source constraints
- a list of artifacts that should be linked to the artifact's failure behavior
- a list of artifacts that should be linked to the artifact's capability requirement

The artifact must be stored in a file named `requirements_LEGAL-02.json` and uploaded to the [LLM Requirements Repository](https://github.com/LAION-AI/llama-index/tree/main/llm-requirements).

## Requirement 1: Legal Information Assistant

The system must provide legal information for a user.

### Source Constraints

1. The source must be public.
2. The source must be available online.
3. The source must be accessible to the user.
4. The source must be free of charge or cost.
5. The source must not contain personal information.
6. The source must not contain sensitive information.
7. The source must not contain confidential information.
8. The source must not contain copyrighted material.

The artifact must be in JSON format. It may include a YAML frontmatter block, but it is not required.

The artifact must be named after the system type and task ID. For example, LEGAL-07.json.

The artifact must be placed in the requirements engineering artifact directory.

The artifact must be submitted as a pull request to this repository.

The artifact must be reviewed by at least one other team member before it is merged into the master branch of this repository.

The artifact must be in a format that is compatible with LLMs.

The artifact must be submitted as a pull request to the [LLM requirements engineering repository](https://github.com/google-research/llm-requirements).

The artifact must include:
- A description of the system.
- A list of capabilities.
- A list of source constraints.
- A list of failure behaviors.
- A list of validation scenarios.
- A list of traceability links to other artifacts.
- A list of runtime signals for production governance.
- An example of a capability requirement sentence.
- An example of a source constraint.
- An example of a failure behavior.
- An example of a validation scenario.
- An example of a traceability link.
- An example of a runtime signal.
- A screenshot of the artifact in LLM requirements engineering.

The artifact must include a description of the LLM system, its capabilities and limitations. It must also include a reference to the relevant evidence. The artifact must include a description of the failure behavior. It must include a description of the validation scenarios. It must include a description of the traceability links. It must include a description of the runtime signals.

The artifact must be in JSON format and must be named after the task ID. For example, the artifact for FIN-05 should be named fin-05.json.

The JSON fields must contain:
- capability_requirement: one precise requirement sentence
- source_constraints: list of allowed evidence/source constraints
- failure_behavior: list of refusal, escalation, privacy, or uncertainty behaviors
- validation_scenarios: list of scenario names or short scenario descriptions
- traceability_links: list of artifacts that should be linked to the requirement
- runtime_signals: list of monitoring signals for production governance

The artifact must be in JSON format. It must contain a single key, "requirements", which is an array of JSON objects. Each object must have the following keys:
- capability_requirement (string): one precise requirement sentence
- source_constraints (array of strings): list of allowed evidence/source constraints
- failure_behavior (array of strings): list of refusal, escalation, privacy, or uncertainty behaviors
- validation_scenarios (array of strings or short scenario descriptions): list of scenario names or short scenario descriptions
- traceability_links (array of artifact identifiers): list of artifacts that should be linked to the requirement
- runtime_signals (array of monitoring signals for production governance): list of monitoring signals for production governance

The artifact must include a description of the system, its capabilities and limitations. It must also include a reference to the LLM model used by the system.

The artifact must include a list of scenarios that should be considered when testing the system. These scenarios should be based on real-world situations. The scenarios should be short and precise. They should not contain personal information or other sensitive data.

The artifact must include a description of how the system handles failure scenarios. It must also include a reference to the LLM model used by the system.

The artifact must include a list of signals that should be monitored during production. These signals should be based on real-world situations. The signals should not contain personal information or other sensitive data.

The artifact must include a description of how the system handles privacy concerns. It must also include a reference to the LLM model used by the system.

The artifact must include a list of scenarios that should be considered when testing the system. These scenarios should be based on real-world situations. The scenarios should not contain personal information or other sensitive data.

The artifact must include a description of how the system handles uncertainty concerns. It must also include a reference to the LLM model used by the system.

The artifact must include a list of scenarios that should be considered when testing the system. These scenarios should be based on real-world situations. The scenarios should not contain personal information or other sensitive data.

The artifact must include:
- a description of the capability requirement
- a list of source constraints (evidence) that are allowed for this requirement
- a list of failure behaviors (refusal, escalation, privacy, or uncertainty) that are disallowed for this requirement
- a list of validation scenarios that should be used to validate this requirement

## Privacy compliance assistant

Privacy compliance assistant is an LLM that handles privacy compliance requests. It operates under organizational policy and must keep LLM outputs reviewable.

The privacy compliance assistant must be able to handle the following scenarios:
- [ ] Requesting approval for transfer of data to a vendor.
- [ ] Requesting escalation to DPO.
- [ ] Requesting privacy impact assessment.
- [ ] Requesting review of outdated evidence.
- [ ] Requesting review of updated evidence.
- [ ] Requesting review of new evidence.
